one_line_definition: Ultimate load capacity of a fastened composite-laminate connection, governed by bearing, shearout, or net-section failure modes
Composite joint failure where laminate crushes around the fastener hole under compressive bearing stress
Joint failure by shear fracture of the laminate between the hole edge and the specimen end
Joint failure by tensile fracture of the net cross-section of the laminate at the fastener hole
Analytical stress-field procedure developed by MCAIR to predict static strength of bolted composite joints
Controlled moisture/temperature pre-conditioning of composite specimens to simulate service environments
Deviations from nominal manufacturing specification (porosity, out-of-round holes, interference fit, broken fibres) that affect joint strength
Thin region near a solid surface where velocity rises from zero (no-slip) to the free-stream value due to viscous effects
Thickness of the equivalent inviscid flow that accounts for the mass flow deficit caused by the boundary layer
Thickness of the equivalent inviscid flow that accounts for the momentum deficit caused by the boundary layer
Ratio of boundary-layer displacement thickness to momentum thickness; rises sharply as flow approaches separation
Detachment of the boundary layer from the surface under an adverse pressure gradient, producing reverse flow
Integral form of the boundary-layer equation relating wall shear stress to growth of momentum thickness along a surface
Mathematical conditions defining the onset of plastic yielding in a material under combined stress states
Critical compressive load at which a slender column undergoes lateral deflection; Pcr = π²EI/(Le)²
Linear damage accumulation rule for fatigue life under variable amplitude loading: Σ(ni/Ni) = 1 at failure
Material property (critical stress intensity factor) characterising resistance to crack propagation
Feedback control law combining proportional, integral, and derivative error terms to regulate a process variable
Graphical technique showing how closed-loop poles move in the s-plane as controller gain varies
Frequency at which an undamped system oscillates freely; ωn = √(k/m) for a simple spring-mass system
Ratio of actual damping to critical damping; determines whether system response is underdamped, critically damped, or overdamped
Natural log of ratio of successive amplitudes in free vibration; δ = 2πζ/√(1−ζ²)
Ratio of steady-state dynamic displacement amplitude to static deflection under harmonic excitation
Ratio of transmitted force (or motion) to applied force (or base motion); key parameter in vibration isolation design
Integral expression for the response of a linear system to an arbitrary forcing function via impulse response function
Method of decoupling MDOF equations of motion using mass-normalized mode shapes (eigenvectors)
Auxiliary mass-spring system attached to a primary structure to suppress vibration at a target frequency
Energy-based method for estimating the fundamental natural frequency of a structure by equating maximum kinetic and potential energies
Discretisation technique that formulates mass and stiffness matrices from element shape functions for vibration analysis
Self-excited lateral vibration of a rotating shaft occurring at speeds near the system's natural frequency
Frequency-domain representation of the mean-square content of a random signal per unit frequency
Standard methods of representing three-dimensional objects as multiple two-dimensional views on a drawing sheet
British and ISO standard for defining, specifying, and graphically representing products, replacing BS 308
System of symbols and rules for specifying allowable variation in form, orientation, location, and runout of features
Rectangular box on engineering drawings containing the tolerance symbol, value, and datum references
State of a feature at its maximum material content (smallest hole or largest shaft); allows bonus tolerance
Theoretically exact point, line, or plane used as the reference for geometric tolerances
Standardised system specifying shaft and hole tolerances by fundamental deviation letter and grade number
Geometric tolerance controlling the location of a feature relative to a datum reference frame
States that a physically complete equation in n variables with k dimensionally independent variables can be rewritten using n−k dimensionless groups
Property of a physical equation whereby both sides and all summed terms share the same dimension, ensuring unit-invariance
Physical property defined by a comparison operation (equality) and an addition operation; measurable in its own units
Physical quantity defined as a product of powers of base quantities; its dimension depends on the unit system chosen
Product of physical variables combined such that the result has dimension unity; the building block of dimensional analysis
Condition under which two physical systems obey the same dimensionless relationships, enabling scale-model testing
Dimensional analysis performed by normalising the governing equations rather than from variable lists alone; can reveal higher-order similarities

concept_term: Principle of absolute significance of relative values
one_line_definition: Physical relationships must remain valid regardless of the choice of base units; the foundation of dimensional homogeneity
